summary: Matched a large slice of common fighter AI/behavior code in `ftCo_0A01.c`, raising the file report to 14.73% matched. The PR replaced many placeholder declarations with typed implementations, added helper inlines, refined fighter/item/map-platform structs and prototypes, and made a small wiki report tooling fix. There were no review comments in the slice, so intent is inferred from the diff and generated match report.
what_changed: Implemented several previously placeholder functions in `ftCo_0A01.c`, including `ftCo_800A0384`, `ftCo_800A05F4`, `ftCo_800A0AF4`, `ftCo_800A2718`, `ftCo_800A4A40`, `ftCo_800A949C`, `ftCo_800B0760`, and `ftCo_800B0AF4`.
Added helper inlines around fighter filtering, stage/platform checks, item-kind checks, Nana/Popo special-low behavior, and common `x1A88` state initialization.

Updated `ftCo_0A01.h` prototypes from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete types for matched functions and dependencies such as `ftCo_800A4A40`, `ftCo_800A2718`, `ftCo_800B0760`, `ftCo_800A5944`, `ftCo_800A5F4C`, `ftCo_800A648C`, `ftCo_800A7AAC`, and `ftCo_800A949C`.

Expanded `struct Fighter_x1A88_t` in `ft/types.h`, typing `x4C` as `void*`, `x50` as `int`, adding a typed `x448` substructure with pad fields, offsets, and facing direction, and changing `x558` from padding to `float`.
Corrected item hitbox-related prototypes: `it_8026C1B4` and `it_80275870` now return `bool`, and `it_8026C1B4` returns the result of `it_80275870`.
Changed `ftCo_800A5944` to operate on `Item*` and check `It_Kind_BombHei`, `It_Kind_G_Shell`, and `It_Kind_R_Shell` instead of comparing fighter motion IDs.
Merged `mp_UnkStruct4` fields into `mp_UnkStruct0`, removed the separate `mp_UnkStruct4`, and updated `mpIsland_8005AC8C` to accept `mp_UnkStruct0*`.
Added includes needed by the new code, including `ftPopo/forward.h`, `ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_1211.h`, `it/inlines.h`, `it/types.h`, `mp/forward.h`, and `ft/types.h`.

Updated `tools/wiki_tu.py` to print human-friendly byte sizes using `humanfriendly.format_size(...).replace('bytes', 'B')` and changed source links from `../../tree/master/src/...` to `../blob/master/src/...`.
smart_moves: Typed dependent APIs and structs in the same PR as the decomp work, avoiding isolated matched code that still depended on `UNK_RET`, `UNK_PARAMS`, or opaque padding.

Reused common helper inlines instead of duplicating repeated state setup and eligibility checks; for example, `inlineI0` centralizes several `x1A88` bitfield resets and is reused by `ftCo_800B0760` and `ftCo_800B1DA0`.
Preserved known compiler-sensitive control flow in `ftCo_800A2718` with explicit `goto` labels and `PAD_STACK`, which is a practical tactic when structured C would disturb matching.

Converted magic fighter motion constants to named enum values where evidence was available, such as `ftCo_MS_Ottotto`, `ftCo_MS_OttottoWait`, `ftCo_MS_FireFlowerShoot`, and `ftCo_MS_FireFlowerShootAir`.
Pulled in Popo/Nana-specific headers only when matching `ftCo_800B0AF4`, making the cross-character dependency explicit rather than leaving the function untyped.
Fixed return-type propagation across item APIs so boolean tests in matched fighter code could compile naturally instead of relying on void or placeholder calls.
decomp_lessons: Large common-fighter files often require cross-system cleanup: matching `ftCo_0A01.c` needed fighter struct layout, item return types, map-platform structs, and Popo motion/action declarations.

When a field starts being used arithmetically or structurally, replace padding with a typed field rather than using `M2C_FIELD`; this PR typed `x1A88.x558` as a `float` and introduced the `x448` pointer substructure.
If a decompiled function tests a callee result, audit the callee and all declarations for return type accuracy; `it_8026C1B4` and `it_80275870` had to become `bool` for item-hitbox checks.
Temporary inline names such as `inlineD0`, `inlineI1`, and `inlineL0` can be used to express matched compiler structure, but they should be kept local and renamed as understanding improves.
Stage-specific behavior can depend on exact internal stage IDs and map structures; `ftCo_800A2718` combines `STORY`, `ZEBES`, `ONETT`, `mpIsland_8005AC8C`, and `Ground_801C5794` checks.
Matching AI/behavior scripts may look like repeated calls to command emitters such as `ftCo_800B46B8` and `ftCo_800B463C`; preserving order, literals, and early returns is central to matching.
naming_conventions: Common fighter symbols retain the `ftCo_` prefix and address-style suffixes where semantic names are not yet known.
Character-specific Popo symbols use the `ftPp_` prefix, e.g. `ftPp_MS_SpecialLw`, `ftPp_MS_SpecialAirLw`, and `ftPp_SpecialLw_Enter`.

Item kinds are expressed with `It_Kind_*` enum names rather than raw integers when known, e.g. `It_Kind_BombHei`, `It_Kind_G_Shell`, `It_Kind_R_Shell`, `It_Kind_Heart`, `It_Kind_Tomato`, and `It_Kind_Foods`.
Fighter motion IDs should use `ftCo_MS_*` names instead of magic numbers when the enum value is known.
Anonymous or partially understood structs keep offset-based field names such as `x1A88`, `x448`, `x558`, and `mp_UnkStruct0`, while comments preserve offsets.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK` in functions such as `ftCo_800A2718`, `ftCo_800A4A40`, `ftCo_800B0760`, and existing callers to satisfy stack layout requirements.
Used explicit `goto` blocks and label-style control flow in `inlineL0` for `ftCo_800A2718`, indicating the original control flow did not naturally structure into clean C without affecting codegen.

Factored compiler-repeated snippets into static inline helpers, including `inlineI0` for state initialization, `inlineI1` for `x1A88` state checks, and `inlineJ0` for Popo/Nana special-low conditions.
Kept exact floating constants and thresholds such as `0.6f`, `0.8f`, `0.9f`, `30.0`, `0.95`, and `0.05` in behavior logic.
Used `GET_FIGHTER`, `GET_ITEM`, `HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters`, and `HSD_GObj_Entities->items` traversal patterns to mirror object-list iteration.

Adjusted local helper names after inserting new inlines to avoid collisions and preserve ordering, e.g. old `inlineF0`/`inlineF1` became `inlineD0`/`inlineD1`, and later helpers shifted to `inlineI*`, `inlineJ0`, and `inlineK0`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for `ftCo_800A2718` to see whether the `goto`-heavy `inlineL0` was later cleaned up or renamed.
Search for `struct Fighter_x1A88_t` and `x448` to find subsequent field-name refinements for Nana/Popo follower state.
Search for `ftCo_800A5944(Item* ip)` to confirm whether its item-kind predicate received a semantic name.
Search for `mp_UnkStruct0` and removed `mp_UnkStruct4` to track map-platform struct consolidation.
Search for `it_8026C1B4` and `it_80275870` to verify all declarations and callers use `bool` consistently.
